channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Kongsberg Q1 orders more than double, eyes US expansion | Breaking Defense
item.description: Across the last quarter, other key contracts included the firm’s joint selection along with Salt Ship Design to deliver a design concept for standardised vessels to the Royal Norwegian Navy.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: May 7, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE Iranian officials and the Trump administration yesterday offered contradictory and rapidly changing…

item.title: 🕵️ AI ‘blueprint’ coming soon to NGA to help ‘operationalize’ GEOINT | Breaking Defense
item.description: In her first major speech since taking the reins at NGA, Lt. Gen. Michelle Bredenkamp also said the agency has stood up a new Rapid Capabilities Office to speed integration of innovative commercial tech.

item.title: 🌏 How China Is Winning the Global AI Race | Foreign Policy
item.description: Cutting-edge U.S. models are too expensive for much of the world.

item.title: 🏴 Vladimir Putin Is Much Weaker Than You Think | Foreign Policy
item.description: Used to outwitting his enemies, the Russian leader is running out of room for maneuver.

item.title: 🏴 Dueling Victory Day ceasefires for war in Ukraine collapse almost immediately | Defense News
item.description: Both sides had proclaimed a ceasefire surrounding the important celebration marking the Soviet Union’s contribution to victory over Nazi Germany.

item.title: ⚛️ The Pentagon Needs a Playbook for Munitions Surge Production | War on the Rocks
item.description: The Tomahawk Land Attack Missiles the U.S. military fired during Operation Epic Fury take months to put on contract and years to produce.Whether driven by U.S. military operations or support to partners, the challenge of quickly replenishing U.S. munitions is not new. Exquisite…

item.title: 🏛️ What 300 Emails Say About Americans and the Army’s Direct Commission Program | War on the Rocks
item.description: For nine months in 2024 and 2025, I had an additional duty — monitoring an inbox that connected potential direct commission candidates with the Army’s individual branches. I served on the Army Reserve’s senior leadership team, helping to stand up a brokerage between mid-career…

item.title: 🏛️ The Army Needs to Build Better Command Posts | War on the Rocks
item.description: It would be odd to see a TOC Mahal in the Russo-Ukrainian War. Often spotted at U.S. Army Combat Training Center rotations or large home-station exercises, TOC Mahals are sprawling tactical operation centers (TOCs) made from a series of connected tents, vehicles, and generators.…

item.title: 🌏 Japan and Australia are perfect partners in the world of AI | ASPI Strategist
item.description: AI is the most consequential technology we will ever create. The visit by Japan’s pro-industry Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to Australia this week was a chance to bind our efforts with those of a natural ...

item.title: 📍 Reasserting Public Scrutiny Over Indonesia’s Foreign Policy | The Diplomat
item.description: At a time when Jakarta's foreign policy decisions are particularly consequential and sometimes controversial, public scrutiny of them has strangely diminished.

item.title: 🌍 Malaysia’s Hotline to Tehran | The Diplomat
item.description: When Malaysian oil tankers became stranded in the Persian Gulf, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim phoned a friend.

item.title: 📍 Why the Rupiah is Weakening | The Diplomat
item.description: Despite what the alarming drop in the value of its currency might suggest, Indonesia is not on the brink of an economic crisis.

item.title: 📍 Thailand Unilaterally Voids Maritime Boundary Agreement With Cambodia | The Diplomat
item.description: The 2001 MoU set up a framework for discussions on joint oil and gas exploration in areas of the Gulf of Thailand claimed by both sides.

item.title: 🌍 War Handed Public Space Back to Iran’s Regime | Foreign Policy
item.description: After years of quiet encroachment by ordinary people, the Islamic Republic reclaims the urban landscape.

item.title: 🏴 This Is Not the World Russia Wants | Foreign Affairs
item.description: A belligerent America is foiling Putin’s strategy.

item.title: 🌏 Philippines Urges Myanmar to Grant ASEAN Special Envoy Access to Aung San Suu Kyi | The Diplomat
item.description: The request came a day ahead of the 48th ASEAN Summit in Cebu, where the Myanmar conflict will once again be high on the agenda.

item.title: 📍 Australia’s security ecosystem needs diversity, not just more data | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Every system operating under finite resources – seemingly every system now – needs to compress its environment: simplify, filter, decide what to pay attention to and what to let go. The fix isn’t to track ...

item.title: 📍 Defense Business Brief: Pitching America first; Visa deal?; Skunk Works exec moves up; plus a little more | Defense One
item.description: Click to read the full story at the source.

item.title: 🔐 Pentagon leaders love agentic AI. But it’s giving cyber criminals nation-state-like powers | Defense One
item.description: As new tools change cybersecurity, just moving faster won’t be enough.

item.title: 🌍 F/A-18 Super Hornet Blasts Blockade-Running Iranian Ship’s Rudder With 20mm Cannon (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The Super Hornet strafing the ship is the latest instance where the Navy used direct fire to disable Iranian ships trying to run the blockade. The post F/A-18 Super Hornet Blasts Blockade-Running Iranian Ship’s Rudder With 20mm Cannon (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 U.S. Might Be Close to a Deal With Iran | Foreign Policy
item.description: A reported memorandum would end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and lay the framework for future nuclear talks.

item.title: 🔐 Cyberattacks are now part of US counterterrorism strategy | Defense One
item.description: The White House says it's trying to deter foreign hackers.

item.title: 📍 The BJP Scores Big in West Bengal | Foreign Policy
item.description: India’s ruling party got a boost from four state elections this week—and unseated a longtime rival.

item.title: 🌏 China Tests a Rare Tool in Its Sanctions Arsenal | Foreign Policy
item.description: Ahead of the Trump-Xi summit, Beijing is pushing back on Washington.

item.title: 🌍 How the Iran war is affecting Latin America and the Caribbean’s economic outlook | Atlantic Council
item.description: The data show how energy and commodity shortages are pushing inflation higher, tightening financial conditions, and slowing growth. The post How the Iran war is affecting Latin America and the Caribbean’s economic outlook appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🔐 Battery-electric freight trains would lift Australia’s energy independence | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia has an obvious way to reduce its vulnerability to disruption of fuel supplies: making battery-electric locomotives the standard means of freight-rail traction and, through resulting cost savings, draw more long-distance goods transportation onto trains. ...

item.title: 🕵️ Space Force boosts contract ceiling for Andromeda space monitoring sats to $6.2B | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Andromeda IDIQ contract vehicle will fund a replacement for the current GSSAP on-orbit inspection satellites as well as the classified SILENTBARKER space surveillance birds.

item.title: 🌍 Washington Is Still Chasing the Perfect War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Iran shows that the delusions that caused Iraq and Afghanistan persist.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran War Is Eroding Israel’s Nuclear Ambiguity | Foreign Policy
item.description: Officially or not, Washington is now breaking the taboo and talking about its ally’s bombs.
